title: Optical densitometry of tyrosine hydroxylase fibers
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"csc22","text":"Protocol for quantifying TH+ fibers in rat dorsal striatum and nucleus accumbens core and shell","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Acquire images with a light microscope of immunostained serial coronal sections covering the caudo-rostral of the brain regions (12 sections/animal for dorsal striatum (dST) and 4-5 sections/animal for nucleus accumbens (NAc) core and shell
Calibrate grey mean values for standard optical density values

Open 8-bit images in ImageJ, trace the region of interest (dST and NAc core and shell), and measure the optical density. Then, in the same image, trace the region of interest in a blank area and measure the optical density.
Calculate the optical density by subtracting the background optical density
creator: Ada Ledonne
